evals.prompt: Hey, we're finally moving our old SVN repo off the company server. The URL is https://svn.internal.corp/repos/billing — it uses the standard trunk/branches/tags layout. We have about 8 years of history and 12 different committers. I want to end up with a proper git repo with all the history, branches, and tags intact. We're on Windows. How do I do this?
evals.expected_output: Skill activates. Guides user through detect phase (via inline PowerShell), then stdlayout selection, authors file creation (with template), metadata/tag/branch-name decisions. Generates inline PowerShell phase snippets (0–8) for execution via `powershell -Command [scriptblock]::Create`, with periodic checkpoints and time budget. Covers verification steps and push-to-remote next steps.

assertions.name: detects_windows_platform
assertions.check: Response references inline PowerShell phases (phases/run-phase.md, lib/core.pslib, scripts/run-phase.pslib) or `powershell -Command` (not migrate.sh or bash or migrate.ps1)

assertions.name: runs_detect_first
assertions.check: Response instructs user to run detect phase (inline PowerShell) before proceeding to migration phases

assertions.name: stdlayout_config_noted
assertions.check: Response guides user to select or confirm stdlayout configuration during the interview

assertions.name: authors_file_guidance
assertions.check: Response explains the authors file format (user = Name <email>) and offers to generate a stub from detect output

assertions.name: phase_snippets_generated
assertions.check: Response generates inline PowerShell phase snippets (or references phases 0–8) for copy-paste into interactive PowerShell

assertions.name: verification_steps_included
assertions.check: Response includes verification commands (git log, git branch, git tag) after all phases complete

evals.prompt: I'm on a Mac and need to migrate an SVN repo to git. The SVN URL is svn+ssh://dev@oldserver.example.com/home/svn/myapp. The repo doesn't have standard trunk/branches/tags — it's all just flat in the root (our old team didn't bother with branching). Also I don't want to keep those git-svn-id lines in the commit messages — this will be a public GitHub repo and they look ugly.
evals.expected_output: Skill activates. Detects macOS → migrate.sh. Warns about bash 4 requirement (brew install bash). Detects single-path layout (no --stdlayout flag). Includes --no-metadata flag in the generated command. Addresses svn+ssh URL format (notes auth may prompt). Shows clean final command.

assertions.name: detects_macos_platform
assertions.check: Response references migrate.sh or bash (not migrate.ps1 or PowerShell) as the primary script

assertions.name: bash4_warning
assertions.check: Response warns about macOS bash 3.2 and mentions brew install bash

assertions.name: no_stdlayout_flag
assertions.check: Generated command does NOT include --stdlayout since this is a flat repo

assertions.name: svnssh_auth_noted
assertions.check: Response mentions that svn+ssh may require authentication or key setup

evals.prompt: We have a huge SVN monorepo at https://svn.company.com/repos/platform with probably 50,000+ revisions. We use trunk/branches/tags. About 30 committers over 15 years. I've already got an authors.txt file ready. I only want to migrate the /platform/services subdirectory, not the whole repo, and I want to exclude the vendor/ directory (it has huge binaries). What's the best approach?
evals.expected_output: Skill activates. Guides through: sub-path migration (URL is https://svn.company.com/repos/platform/services), authors-file usage, --ignore-paths 'vendor/' to exclude vendor dir, large-repo warning (50K revisions = slow, maybe run overnight), and notes the path-filter regex is ref-root-relative (not repo-root-relative like git-svn).

assertions.name: subpath_url_guidance
assertions.check: Response explains the SVN URL should point to the services subdirectory, not the repo root

assertions.name: ignore_paths_flag
assertions.check: Generated command includes --ignore-paths with a pattern to exclude vendor/

assertions.name: large_repo_warning
assertions.check: Response warns that 50K+ revisions will take significant time

assertions.name: path_filter_base_noted
assertions.check: Response notes that ignore-paths patterns are matched against ref-root-relative paths (different from git-svn)
